title: senecio nigrescens
intro: Senecio nigrescens, also known as Black Kohia or Black Groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to New Zealand. Traditionally used in Maori medicine, Senecio nigrescens has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and expectorant, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Senecio nigrescens include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a digestive aid. Senecio nigrescens can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
